attestation.statement: Clinician (clinical sign-off authority) confirmed having clinically reviewed all 50 CVD (Cardiovascular) answer keys in this batch and attests them clinically correct, and authorised ingest.
attestation.recorded_by: claude-opus-4-8 (agent, on clinician's explicit written confirmation in-session)
attestation.scope: all CVD casebundles in this batch (n=50)

codes_manifest.display: Phlebitis and thrombophlebitis of superficial vessels of lower extremities
Varicose veins of lower extremities without ulcer or inflammation

transform_flags: Provisional case_id SEQ (SPEC-VASC-03-00245) is provisional — maintainer to confirm/reassign within the SPEC-VASC-03 bucket; ingest --reseq assigns the real sequence.
Age inferred as ~52 (adult) — the source note states no age; please confirm on clinician review.
Sex inferred as female — the source note states no sex; inferred from varicose-vein epidemiology only, please confirm on clinician review.

Telehealth reprojection applied: the in-person clinician's palpation of the cord, Homan's sign, pedal-pulse check, vitals, and clinical exclusion of DVT/cellulitis are sealed into nodes 10/11 and telehealth_limits; the presentation layer was de-anchored to the pre-diagnosis patient view. Video-observable/patient-reported findings only were placed in 01.objective_data_offered.

Baseline triage tier reprojected to T3 (urgent same-day in-person assessment): the final diagnosis is benign superficial thrombophlebitis, but telehealth cannot exclude the dangerous mimic (DVT) that the source clinician excluded in person, so the honest AI tier is urgent same-day in-person assessment with duplex, not routine care.
Ibuprofen AMT candidate code is the ibuprofen substance SNOMED concept as a placeholder candidate — unverified pending terminology receipt.

difficulty_tier set to red_herring_laden (multiple must-exclude mimics: DVT, cellulitis, PAD) and diagnosis_category important_not_to_miss; the id difficulty ordinal is 03, but case_metadata is authoritative.

ingest.hashing: SHA-256 over canonical (JSON 2-space + trailing newline) bytes of each split node
